channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Supreme Court confirms legality of preventive detention of actor Ranya Rao and associate under gold smuggling charges
item.description: The Supreme Court of India has upheld the preventive detention of Kannada film actor Harshavardhini Ranya Rao and her associate Sahil Jain in an alleged gold smuggling case. The detention was ordered ...

item.title: South Africa Must Strengthen Material Sovereignty Amid Global Power Shifts
item.description: Faiez Jacobs argues that the ongoing US-Israel-Iran conflict is more than a distant Middle Eastern crisis; it is a demonstration of how global power operates through control over energy, trade routes,...

item.title: Commonwealth mission concludes Tanzania visit after talks to advance democratic governance
item.description: The Special Envoy of the Commonwealth Secretary-General and former President of Malawi, Lazarus McCarthy Chakwera, has ended his nine-day official visit to Tanzania on April 16, 2026. His departure th...

item.title: Abel Mutua’s daughter reflects on cost of upbringing after financial literacy lesson
item.description: The daughter of Kenyan filmmaker Abel Mutua, Stephanie Mumbus, has sparked conversation online after sharing her reaction to a financial literacy assignment from school. According to a video shared by...

item.title: KUCCPS Clarifies 2025 University Placement Cut-off Points Ahead of Deadline
item.description: The Kenya University and Colleges Central Placement Service (KUCCPS) has issued clarification regarding the ongoing placement process for the 2026/2027 cohort. The application portal for degree and TV...

item.title: Authorities act against unhygienic poultry waste dumping in Stanger
item.description: An illegal fertiliser operation in Stanger has resulted in a severe odour and fly infestation, causing significant discomfort to local residents. According to Aresh Singh, chairman of NGS Odour and En...

item.title: Sudan’s Long War: Causes, Players, and Humanitarian Fallout
item.description: The war in Sudan has entered its fourth year as of April 15, 2026, marking one of the worst humanitarian crises globally. The conflict pits the Sudanese army led by General Abdel Fattah al-Burhan agai...

item.title: Julius Malema handed five-year jail term for 2018 rally firearm incident
item.description: South African opposition leader Julius Malema, aged 45 and founder of the Economic Freedom Fighters (EFF) party, was sentenced to five years in prison by a magistrate's court in KuGompo City, formerly...

item.title: Turkiye mourns after school shooting claims lives of children and teacher
item.description: In southern Turkiye, the nation is grappling with shock and grief following a tragic school shooting that claimed the lives of eight children aged 10 and 11, along with their teacher. The attack was c...

item.title: Asian Bishops Urge Dialogue and Peace in Middle East Crisis
item.description: Cardinal Pablo Virgilio David, together with other Asian bishops from the Federation of Asian Bishops’ Conferences (FABC), is calling for an end to hostilities in the Middle East. The bishops stressed...

item.title: UPSC CDS 1 2026 Official Answer Key Available for Candidates
item.description: The Union Public Service Commission (UPSC) has officially released the answer key for CDS 1 2026 examination. Candidates who appeared for the Combined Defence Services (CDS) exam can now verify their ...

item.title: Julius Malema Dismisses Lebo Keswa's Ghostwriting Allegations from ANC Days
item.description: A public spat erupted online between Economic Freedom Fighters (EFF) leader Julius Malema and businesswoman Lebo Keswa after she claimed to have worked as his ghostwriter during his time in the Africa...

item.title: Police search for suspect following fatal Lambton College shooting
item.description: Police in Sarnia, Ontario, are actively searching for 24-year-old Kyaw 'Chin' Doe, wanted in connection with a fatal shooting at Lambton College on April 10. The incident resulted in the death of 20-y...

item.title: New CIDRA Research Partnership to Support Tanzania’s Dira 2050 Goals
item.description: A fresh research partnership bringing together 13 universities from Africa and Europe is set to play a big role in pushing forward Tanzania’s Vision 2050 development plans, also known as Dira 2050. Th...

item.title: Daily Superquiz and Mini Crossword for April 17
item.description: This article presents the daily Superquiz feature published for Friday, April 17, offering readers a chance to test their general knowledge and problem-solving skills. Compiled by Michelle Stillman, t...

item.title: PM Modi Assures Fair Delimitation Exercise and Supreme Court Grants Relief to Eligible Bengal Voters

item.title: East African Nations to Finalise Afcon 2027 Preparations Roadmap Next Week
item.description: The governments of Tanzania, Kenya and Uganda are set to convene next week to agree on a joint roadmap for hosting the 2027 Africa Cup of Nations (Afcon). The meeting, named the 'Pamoja' summit, will ...

item.title: Police report on youth altercation in Guelph
item.description: On April 16, 2026, local authorities in Guelph, Ontario, reported that approximately twelve youths were involved in a physical altercation in the city. The incident prompted a police response to ensur...

item.title: Nigeria security forces on alert over planned attacks on Abuja airport and prison
item.description: Security agencies for Nigeria have put themselves on high alert after intelligence reports pointed to possible attacks on key facilities in Abuja and Niger State. The warning, carried in an internal m...

item.title: Dr. Amir Levine Explains How to Develop a Secure Attachment Style
